citations.narrative:
In the outdoor enclosure for the black vulture there were items that could potentially harm and injure the animal. There
was a broken piece of a mirror that was provided as enrichment. There were cords from the heating source within the
enclosure that hung low enough for the animal to come in contact with and two heating lamps that the animal could
access that could potentially harm the vulture. The primary enclosures must be kept in good repair to protect the birds
from injury by removing items that are harmful and in disrepair. Correct by February 12, 2026.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
The outdoor enclosure for a black vulture had the remains of old carcasses from feeding and enrichment that had not
been removed. The den was also dirty and it didn't appear to have been cleaned or managed. The facility stated that it
had been a month since it was last cleaned. There were deposited materials from an alligator pinata used for enrichment
that had been provided in September. The old remains of the enrichment were scattered across the floor of the enclosure.
Food waste and deposited materials must be removed from primary enclosures, as often as necessary, to prevent
excessive accumulation of feces and food waste, and to reduce disease hazards, insects, pests, and odors. Correct by
February 12, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Attending Veterinarian.
n

narrative:
3.153(a)(1)(ii) 
   
Primary enclosures. 
In the outdoor enclosure for the black vulture there were items that could potentially harm and injure the animal. There 
was a broken piece of a mirror that was provided as enrichment. There were cords from the heating source within the 
enclosure that hung low enough for the animal to come in contact with and two heating lamps that the animal could 
access that could potentially harm the vulture. The primary enclosures must be kept in good repair to protect the birds 
from injury by removing items that are harmful and in disrepair. Correct by February 12, 2026. 
3.158(a)(1) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
The outdoor enclosure for a black vulture had the remains of old carcasses from feeding and enrichment that had not 
been removed. The den was also dirty and it didn't appear to have been cleaned or managed. The facility stated that it 
had been a month since it was last cleaned. There were deposited materials from an alligator pinata used for enrichment 
that had been provided in September. The old remains of the enrichment were scattered across the floor of the enclosure. 
Food waste and deposited materials must be removed from primary enclosures, as often as necessary, to prevent 
excessive accumulation of feces and food waste, and to reduce disease hazards, insects, pests, and odors. Correct by 
February 12, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Attending Veterinarian.
